title: Quantitative Immunoblot Analysis of LRRK1 Signalling Pathway
author: Asad Malik

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note : To ensure LRRK1 activation in cells, we recommend treating cells for at least 00:30:00 ± PMA (a PKC activator) at a final concentration of 100 ng/mL (or equivalent volume of DMSO) before lysis. Quickly rinse cells in the tissue culture dish by carefully pouring Room temperature culture media without Foetal bovine serum (FBS) into the dish.

steps.title: Pour off media from the culture...
steps.instruction: Pour off media from the culture dish and completely aspirate any residual media. Immediately add freshly prepared ice-cold lysis buffer, ensuring that the entire surface is covered by lysis buffer. Note : The amount of lysis buffer to use will depend on cell type and cell confluency. As a guideline, use 0.1 mL to 0.2 mL of lysis buffer for each well of a 6-well plate, 0.5 mL for a 10- cm dish and 1 mL for a 15-cm dish. It is preferable to aim for protein concentrations of a least 0.75 µg/µL as this will enable the appropriate amount of protein to be loaded onto a gel as detailed below.

substeps.instruction: Pour off media from the culture dish and completely aspirate any residual media. Immediately add freshly prepared ice-cold lysis buffer, ensuring that the entire surface is covered by lysis buffer. Note : The amount of lysis buffer to use will depend on cell type and cell confluency. As a guideline, use
mL of lysis buffer for each well of a 6-well plate,
mL for a 10- cm dish and 1 mL for a 15-cm dish. It is preferable to aim for protein concentrations of a least
µg/µL as this will enable the appropriate amount of protein to be loaded onto a gel as detailed below.

steps.title: Immediately transfer the cell dishes to ice
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer the cell dishes to ice.

steps.title: Scrape the cells on the dish...
steps.instruction: Scrape the cells on the dish using a cell lifter (Sigma-Aldrich CLS3008, or equivalent) to ensure all cells are detached from the dish.

steps.title: Using a pipette, transfer cell lysate...
steps.instruction: Using a pipette, transfer cell lysate to an Eppendorf tube On ice . Note : For non-adherent cells, transfer cells to a Falcon tube and pellet by centrifugation at 180 x g, °C, 3 minutes ; wash cells once with Room temperature culture media without FBS and pellet again as above. Discard supernatant and add freshly prepared ice-cold lysis buffer. Immediately transfer the Falcon containing the cell pellet to ice.

steps.title: Leave samples On ice for 00:20:00...
steps.instruction: Leave samples On ice for 00:20:00 to allow for efficient lysis.

steps.title: Clarify lysates by centrifugation at 20800...
steps.instruction: Clarify lysates by centrifugation at 20800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Transfer the supernatants into new Eppendorf...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatants into new Eppendorf tubes and discard the pellet. Keep the tubes On ice . Note : Cell lysates can be snap frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at -80 °C for future use.

steps.title: Determine the protein concentration of cell...
steps.instruction: Determine the protein concentration of cell or tissue lysates by Bradford assay according to the manufacturer’s instructions, performing measurements in triplicate. Note: Ensure the concentration of the samples is in the linear range for the Bradford assay. If it isn’t, prepare appropriate dilutions in water of each lysate. Generally, protein concentrations of near confluent cells lysed as described above should range from 0.5 to 5 mg/ml (depending on cell type), while tissues lysed as described above should result in protein concentrations of at least 2 mg/ml.

steps.title: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve...
steps.instruction: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve the same protein concentration for all samples (ideally, 0.5 µg/µL to 2 µg/µL , depending on the sample at the lowest concentration) by combining the cell lysate with lysis buffer. Add a quarter of a volume of 4X SDS/LDS loading buffer freshly supplemented with beta-mercaptoethanol (i.e. for 7.5 µL of lysate/lysis buffer mix, add 2.5 µL of loading buffer). Mix by vortexing.

substeps.instruction: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve the same protein concentration for all samples (ideally,

µg/µL to 2 µg/µL , depending on the sample at the lowest concentration) by combining the cell lysate with lysis buffer. Add a quarter of a volume of 4X SDS/LDS loading buffer freshly supplemented with beta-mercaptoethanol (i.e. for
µL of lysate/lysis buffer mix, add
µL of loading buffer). Mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate samples for 00:05:00 at 70...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 00:05:00 at 70 °C heating block before immunoblot analysis.

steps.title: Load samples onto a NuPAGE 4–12%...
steps.instruction: Load samples onto a NuPAGE 4–12% Bis–Tris Midi Gel (ThermoFisherScientific, Cat#WG1402BOX or Cat#WG1403BOX), or a self-cast 10% Bis-Tris gel, alongside pre-stained molecular weight markers (ranging from 10 kDa to 250 kDa). Rinse wells carefully with running buffer before loading samples. Notes : The amount of protein loaded for each sample ranges from 10 µg to 40 µg , depending on the cell/tissue type and the protein(s) of interest. For cell lines like mouse embryonic fibroblasts, A549 cells and cells isolated from human peripheral blood (monocytes, neutrophils), we recommend loading 10 µg to 15 µg of protein for each cell extract for optimal signal; for mouse tissues like brain, spleen, kidney, lung and intestine we recommend loading 30 µg to 40 µg of protein for each tissue extract for optimal signal. Be aware of maximum loading capacity of each well as per manufacturer’s instructions and take care not to overload wells. If multiple gels are used for each set of experimental samples, an internal loading control should also be included for subsequent data normalization.

steps.title: Electrophorese samples at 130V with MOPS...
steps.instruction: Electrophorese samples at 130V with MOPS SDS running buffer for 02:00:00 or until the blue dye runs off the gel.

steps.title: Equilibrate the gel, one piece of...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the gel, one piece of nitrocellulose membrane (GE Healthcare, Amersham Protran Supported 0.45 μm NC) and two pieces of filter paper (WhatmanTM 3MM Chr Chromatography Paper, or equivalent) (all of the same size as the gel) by pre-soaking them in transfer buffer.

steps.title: Assemble the gel and membrane transfer...
steps.instruction: Assemble the gel and membrane transfer stack in a tray filled with transfer buffer to ensure that all components are submerged during the assembling. Place one sponge pad inside the cassette holder (on the side that will be facing the cathode). Place one piece of filter paper on top of the sponge pad, followed by the gel, nitrocellulose membrane, another piece of filter paper and another sponge pad. Note : Carefully remove any air bubbles between layers using a roller after adding each layer.

steps.title: Carefully close the cassette holder and...
steps.instruction: Carefully close the cassette holder and insert it in the transfer tank. Fill the tank with transfer buffer.

steps.title: Electrophoretically transfer proteins from gel onto...
steps.instruction: Electrophoretically transfer proteins from gel onto a nitrocellulose membrane at 100 V (constant voltage) for 01:30:00 On ice using a wet transfer system.

steps.title: After transfer, stain membranes with Ponceau...
steps.instruction: After transfer, stain membranes with Ponceau solution to assess transfer efficiency and general quality of the samples. If an image is required for record, the Ponceau-stained membraned can be scanned.

steps.title: Each membrane can be divided into...
steps.instruction: Each membrane can be divided into three sections by two horizontal cuts (one cut just above the 75 kDa ladder band and another cut between the 37.5 kDa and 25 kDa ladder bands) (Figure 1): ‘top section’ (from the top of the membrane to the 75 kDa marker) - to be probed for LRRK1 ‘middle section’ (between the 75 kDa and the 37.5 kDa marker) - to be probed for GAPDH/tubulin (loading control) ‘bottom section’ (from the 37.5 kDa marker to the bottom of the membrane) - to be probed for pS72-Rab7A/Rab7A Figure 1. Ponceau-stained membrane. Notes : When immunoblotting using anti-phospho(Ser)-PKC Substrates, it is preferable to blot the entire membrane to visualise all induced PKC substrates. The same blot can be simultaneously be blotted with a loading control like alpha tubulin or glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase (GAPDH) and pT202/Y204 ERK1/2 (to confirm that stimulation of cells with PMA has worked).

substeps.instruction: Each membrane can be divided into three sections by two horizontal cuts (one cut just above the 75 kDa ladder band and another cut between the
kDa and 25 kDa ladder bands) (Figure 1): ‘top section’ (from the top of the membrane to the 75 kDa marker) - to be probed for LRRK1 ‘middle section’ (between the 75 kDa and the
kDa marker) - to be probed for GAPDH/tubulin (loading control) ‘bottom section’ (from the

kDa marker to the bottom of the membrane) - to be probed for pS72-Rab7A/Rab7A Figure 1. Ponceau-stained membrane. Notes : When immunoblotting using anti-phospho(Ser)-PKC Substrates, it is preferable to blot the entire membrane to visualise all induced PKC substrates. The same blot can be simultaneously be blotted with a loading control like alpha tubulin or glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase (GAPDH) and pT202/Y204 ERK1/2 (to confirm that stimulation of cells with PMA has worked).

steps.title: Destain membranes from Step 19 by...
steps.instruction: Destain membranes from Step 19 by washing with TBS-T and incubate in blocking solution for at least 00:15:00 at Room temperature on a see-saw rocker.

steps.title: Rinse the membrane in TBS-T and...
steps.instruction: Rinse the membrane in TBS-T and incubate Overnight at 4 °C with primary antibodies (diluted in 5% (w/v) BSA in TBS-T to their working concentration – Table 1), as follows: Table 1: %s %s ­Antibody Target Company Cat. number Host species Dilution pS72 Rab7A Abcam Inc. MJF-38, Clone 1 Rabbit 1 mg/ml Rab7A (Total) Sigma R8779 Mouse 1 mg/ml pT202/Y204 ERK1/2 Cell Signalling Technology #9101 Rabbit 1 mg/ml Phospho-(Ser) PKC Substrate Antibody Cell Signalling Technology #2261 Rabbit 1 mg/ml LRRK1 (total) (C-terminus) MRC-PPU Reagents and Services, University of Dundee S405C Sheep 1 mg/ml GAPDH Antibodies Inc./NeuroMab sc-32233 Mouse 1:5,000 alpha-tubulin Cell Signaling Technology 3873 Mouse 1:5,000

steps.title: After incubation with primary antibodies, wash...
steps.instruction: After incubation with primary antibodies, wash membranes in TBS-T on a see-saw rocker (3 washes, 5-10 minutes per wash).

steps.title: Incubate membranes with near-infrared fluorescent dye-labelled...
steps.instruction: Incubate membranes with near-infrared fluorescent dye-labelled secondary antibodies (diluted to the working concentration: 1:20,000) for 01:00:00 at Room temperature on a see-saw rocker. Note : If multiplexing primary antibodies, use secondary antibodies labelled with spectrally distinct near-infrared fluorescent dyes. Generally, we use IRDye 800CW (800 nm channel) secondary antibodies for the phospho-antibodies multiplexed with IRDye 680LT (680 nm channel) secondary antibodies for the corresponding total antibody. Table 2 lists the near-infrared fluorescent dye-labelled secondary antibodies used in our lab. Table 2: %s %s Secondary Antibodies Company Cat. number Notes goat anti-mouse IRDye 680LT LI-COR 926-68020 goat anti-mouse IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32210 goat anti-rabbit IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32211 donkey anti-mouse IRDye 680LT LI-COR 926-68022 donkey anti-mouse IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32212 donkey anti-rabbit IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32213 donkey anti-goat IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32214 Reacts with Sheep primary Abs

steps.title: Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T on...
steps.instruction: Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T on a see-saw rocker (4 washes, 10-15 minutes per wash).

steps.title: Protein bands are acquired via near...
steps.instruction: Protein bands are acquired via near infrared fluorescent detection using the Odyssey CLx Imaging System and the signal intensity quantified using the Image Studio Software. Note : To control for inter-gel variability, the signal intensity of each band can be normalised against the control sample loaded in each gel of a set of experiments.

steps.title: Analyse immunoblotting data using a software...
steps.instruction: Analyse immunoblotting data using a software for statistical analysis (Graphpad Prism, or equivalent). Figure 2. Representative results of quantitative immunoblotting analysis of pSer72 Rab7A/total Rab7A and total LRRK1 levels performed in extracts from wild-type (WT) and homozygous LRRK1 knock-out (KO) mouse embryonic fibroblasts treated +/- PKC activator PMA (100 ng/ml, 30 min) according to the protocol described here.
